title: Clinical Blink Reflex
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ef4se","text":"Clinical protocol to evoke the blink reflex. This protocol describes how to define blink reflex thresholds, following the clinical technique and guidelines from Linköping University Hospital. steps: Start Natus Elite - Synergy EMG software program.
Choose the protocol named "Blinkreflex". Choose "New nerve" and side (right or left eye).
The subject should be placed comfortable in a seated or supine position. Place the ground electrode in the middle of the forehead on the subject.
Place the anode on the orbicularis oculi muscle under the eye. Place the cathode on the orbicularis oculi muscle at the outer corner of the eye.
Place the stimulating electrode against the orbital nerve at the eyebrow.
To electrically stimulate, press the "Switch" button.

Start with a low stimulus intensity of 1mA and slowly increase in 1mA increments until a blink reflex can be visualized. Wait randomly for at least 20s in-between stimuli to evoke a surprising effect.
Repeat the stimulus at reflex intensity 3 times.
The stimulus intensity at which a reflex can be successfully evoked 3 times, is taken as the Nociceptive withdrawal reflex threshold.
creator: Oumie Thorell
